title: Origami tulip and stem
materials: origami-washi (2 squares: one 15cm for the flower, one 10cm × 15cm rectangle for the stem)
optional: a thin dowel or skewer inside the stem for rigidity
sources: Traditional origami tulip, public domain. Documented in the Froebel kindergarten origami tradition (pre-1928) and in late-Meiji Japanese origami primers via Internet Archive. The tulip model is among the simplest closed-form 3D origami flowers.
notes: A two-part model: the tulip head is folded from a square using the BALLOON BASE construction (all valley and mountain folds, no squash or petal fold required — v1 renderer safe). Construction: fold square diagonally in half to triangle, fold in half again to small triangle, unfold to the first triangle, fold each lower corner of the long edge up to the apex (two valley folds), flip over, fold the bottom flap up to 2/3 height, fold up again to lock, then blow into the bottom hole to inflate. The stem is folded from a small rectangle into a narrow tube. Cover the origamiFoldDiagram for both the flower and the stem. Do NOT use the preliminary-base/petal-fold construction — that requires squash folds (unsupported in v1 renderer).
